# dbx CLI Reference
# Auto-generated — do not edit manually.
# Source: go run ./cmd/docgen

## dbxcli
  dbx — multi-database management platform
  dbx is a multi-database management platform supporting Oracle and PostgreSQL.
  
  Commands use named parameters (emcli-style):
    dbxcli <domain> <action> entity_name=<name> entity_type=<type> [key=value ...]
  Flags:
    --format  output format: table, json, yaml (default: table)

## dbxcli db
  Oracle database read-only operations
  Read-only Oracle database operations covering sessions, tablespaces,
  users, schemas, SQL execution, redo logs, undo, parameters, and advisors.
  
  Requires a target with an Oracle database endpoint configured.
  Flags:
    --target  target name (from ~/.dbx/targets/)
  Inherited Flags:
    --format  output format: table, json, yaml

## dbxcli db advisor
  Oracle advisor operations
  Query Oracle built-in advisor recommendations (DBA_ADVISOR_RECOMMENDATIONS). Read-only.
  Inherited Flags:
    --format  output format: table, json, yaml
    --target  target name (from ~/.dbx/targets/)

## dbxcli db advisor segment
  Show segment advisor recommendations
  Show segment advisor recommendations for table/index shrink, compression, and space reclamation.
  Examples:
    dbxcli db advisor segment --target prod-db

## dbxcli db advisor sql-tuning
  List SQL tuning advisor tasks
  List SQL tuning advisor tasks and their recommendations (DBA_ADVISOR_TASKS).
  Examples:
    dbxcli db advisor sql-tuning --target prod-db

## dbxcli db parameter
  Oracle init parameter operations
  Query Oracle initialization parameters (V$PARAMETER, V$SYSTEM_PARAMETER). Read-only — use EE for SET/RESET.
  Inherited Flags:
    --format  output format: table, json, yaml
    --target  target name (from ~/.dbx/targets/)

## dbxcli db parameter describe
  Describe a specific parameter
  Show full details for a parameter including description, default, range, and whether it requires restart.
  Examples:
    dbxcli db parameter describe name=sga_target --target prod-db

## dbxcli db parameter hidden
  List hidden underscore parameters
  List hidden (underscore-prefixed) parameters. Use with caution — these are unsupported by Oracle.
  Examples:
    dbxcli db parameter hidden --target prod-db

## dbxcli db parameter list
  List all visible parameters
  List all non-hidden initialization parameters with current values and whether they are modified.
  Examples:
    dbxcli db parameter list --target prod-db
      dbxcli db parameter list --target prod-db --format json

## dbxcli db parameter modified
  List non-default parameters
  List only parameters that have been changed from their default values.
  Examples:
    dbxcli db parameter modified --target prod-db

## dbxcli db redo
  Oracle redo log operations
  Query Oracle redo log groups and switch history (V$LOG, V$LOG_HISTORY). Read-only.
  Inherited Flags:
    --format  output format: table, json, yaml
    --target  target name (from ~/.dbx/targets/)

## dbxcli db redo list
  List redo log groups
  List all redo log groups with status, size, member count, and sequence number.
  Examples:
    dbxcli db redo list --target prod-db

## dbxcli db redo switch-history
  Show log switch frequency
  Show redo log switch frequency per hour — useful for sizing redo logs.
  Examples:
    dbxcli db redo switch-history --target prod-db

## dbxcli db schema
  Oracle schema browser
  Browse Oracle schemas and their objects (DBA_OBJECTS, DBA_TABLES, DBA_INDEXES). Read-only.
  Inherited Flags:
    --format  output format: table, json, yaml
    --target  target name (from ~/.dbx/targets/)

## dbxcli db schema describe
  Describe a specific object
  Show DDL and metadata for a specific database object (table, index, view, package, etc.).
  Examples:
    dbxcli db schema describe owner=HR object_name=EMPLOYEES --target prod-db

## dbxcli db schema list
  List schemas with object counts
  List all schemas with counts of tables, indexes, views, packages, and other objects.
  Examples:
    dbxcli db schema list --target prod-db

## dbxcli db schema objects
  List objects in a schema
  List all objects in a given schema filtered by object type.
  Examples:
    dbxcli db schema objects owner=HR --target prod-db
      dbxcli db schema objects owner=HR object_type=TABLE --target prod-db

## dbxcli db session
  Oracle session operations
  Query and inspect Oracle database sessions (V$SESSION). Read-only — no session modification.
  Inherited Flags:
    --format  output format: table, json, yaml
    --target  target name (from ~/.dbx/targets/)

## dbxcli db session describe
  Describe a session by SID
  Show detailed information for a specific session including SQL, wait event, and program.
  Examples:
    dbxcli db session describe sid=142 --target prod-db

## dbxcli db session list
  List active user sessions
  List all active user sessions from V$SESSION excluding background processes.
  Examples:
    dbxcli db session list --target prod-db
      dbxcli db session list --target prod-db --format json

## dbxcli db session top-waiters
  Show top sessions by wait time
  Show sessions with the highest cumulative wait time, useful for identifying performance bottlenecks.
  Examples:
    dbxcli db session top-waiters --target prod-db

## dbxcli db sql
  Read-only SQL execution
  Execute read-only SQL statements against an Oracle database.
  Only SELECT statements are permitted — DML/DDL is blocked by the ReadOnlyGuard.
  Inherited Flags:
    --format  output format: table, json, yaml
    --target  target name (from ~/.dbx/targets/)

## dbxcli db sql exec
  Execute a read-only SELECT statement
  Execute a SELECT query and return results. DML/DDL statements are rejected.
  Examples:
    dbxcli db sql exec query="SELECT sysdate FROM dual" --target prod-db
      dbxcli db sql exec query="SELECT username, account_status FROM dba_users" --target prod-db --format json

## dbxcli db sql explain
  Generate execution plan for a SELECT
  Generate EXPLAIN PLAN output for a SELECT statement without executing it.
  Examples:
    dbxcli db sql explain query="SELECT * FROM hr.employees WHERE department_id = 10" --target prod-db

## dbxcli db tablespace
  Oracle tablespace operations
  Query Oracle tablespace metadata, usage metrics, and datafile details (DBA_TABLESPACES, DBA_DATA_FILES). Read-only.
  Inherited Flags:
    --format  output format: table, json, yaml
    --target  target name (from ~/.dbx/targets/)

## dbxcli db tablespace describe
  Describe tablespace datafiles
  Show datafiles belonging to a tablespace with sizes, autoextend status, and paths.
  Examples:
    dbxcli db tablespace describe name=USERS --target prod-db

## dbxcli db tablespace list
  List tablespaces with usage metrics
  List all tablespaces with total size, used space, free space, and percentage utilization.
  Examples:
    dbxcli db tablespace list --target prod-db
      dbxcli db tablespace list --target prod-db --format json

## dbxcli db tablespace usage
  Show aggregated tablespace usage summary
  Show aggregated storage utilization across all tablespaces with alert thresholds.
  Examples:
    dbxcli db tablespace usage --target prod-db

## dbxcli db undo
  Oracle undo/rollback operations
  Query Oracle undo tablespace usage and rollback segment details (V$UNDOSTAT, DBA_UNDO_EXTENTS). Read-only.
  Inherited Flags:
    --format  output format: table, json, yaml
    --target  target name (from ~/.dbx/targets/)

## dbxcli db undo list
  List undo tablespace usage
  Show undo tablespace utilization including active, unexpired, and expired extents.
  Examples:
    dbxcli db undo list --target prod-db

## dbxcli db undo segments
  Show undo segment details
  Show individual rollback segment status, size, and transaction counts.
  Examples:
    dbxcli db undo segments --target prod-db

## dbxcli db user
  Oracle user operations
  Query Oracle database users, roles, privileges, and profiles (DBA_USERS, DBA_ROLE_PRIVS). Read-only.
  Inherited Flags:
    --format  output format: table, json, yaml
    --target  target name (from ~/.dbx/targets/)

## dbxcli db user describe
  Describe user with roles and privileges
  Show detailed user information including granted roles, system/object privileges, and quotas.
  Examples:
    dbxcli db user describe username=SCOTT --target prod-db

## dbxcli db user list
  List database users
  List all database users with account status, default tablespace, and profile.
  Examples:
    dbxcli db user list --target prod-db

## dbxcli db user profiles
  List database profiles
  List all database profiles with their resource limits and password parameters.
  Examples:
    dbxcli db user profiles --target prod-db

## dbxcli license
  Manage dbx license
  Manage the dbx license. Licenses are Ed25519-signed JWT tokens stored at ~/.dbx/license.jwt.
  Without a license, only OSS (Community) features are available.
  Inherited Flags:
    --format  output format: table, json, yaml

## dbxcli license activate
  Activate a license file
  Activate a license JWT file. The file is validated, copied to ~/.dbx/license.jwt, and EE features are unlocked.
  Flags:
    --file  path to license JWT file
  Examples:
    dbxcli license activate --file /path/to/license.jwt

## dbxcli license status
  Show license status
  Show current license status including tier, entity count, expiry date, and grace period.
  Examples:
    dbxcli license status

## dbxcli linux
  Oracle Linux host management over SSH
  Oracle Linux system management operations executed over SSH.
  Covers package management, kernel parameters, storage/LVM, network, and security.
  
  Requires a target with SSH endpoint configured (oracle_host entity_type).
  Flags:
    --target  target name (from ~/.dbx/targets/)
  Inherited Flags:
    --format  output format: table, json, yaml

## dbxcli linux kernel
  Kernel parameter management
  Kernel parameter management via sysctl and /proc filesystem over SSH.
  Inherited Flags:
    --format  output format: table, json, yaml
    --target  target name (from ~/.dbx/targets/)

## dbxcli linux kernel hugepages
  Set hugepages count (confirm-gated)

## dbxcli linux kernel info
  Show OS/kernel info (uname)

## dbxcli linux kernel param-list
  List all sysctl parameters

## dbxcli linux kernel param-set
  Set a sysctl parameter (confirm-gated)

## dbxcli linux network
  Network diagnostics
  Network diagnostics via ip, nmcli, chronyc, and ss over SSH.
  Inherited Flags:
    --format  output format: table, json, yaml
    --target  target name (from ~/.dbx/targets/)

## dbxcli linux network bond-status
  Show network bond status

## dbxcli linux network dns-check
  Check DNS resolver configuration

## dbxcli linux network nic-list
  List NICs with addresses

## dbxcli linux network ntp-status
  Check NTP synchronization

## dbxcli linux package
  RPM/DNF package management
  RPM package queries and DNF install/update operations via SSH (rpm, dnf commands).
  Inherited Flags:
    --format  output format: table, json, yaml
    --target  target name (from ~/.dbx/targets/)

## dbxcli linux package info
  Show package details
  Show detailed information for a specific RPM package.
  Examples:
    dbxcli linux package info name=oracle-database-ee-19c --target db01

## dbxcli linux package install
  Install a package via DNF (confirm-gated)
  Install a package using dnf. Requires confirmation before execution.
  Examples:
    dbxcli linux package install name=oracle-database-preinstall-19c --target db01

## dbxcli linux package list
  List installed RPM packages
  List all installed RPM packages with version and architecture.
  Examples:
    dbxcli linux package list --target web01

## dbxcli linux package update
  Update a package via DNF (confirm-gated)
  Update a package using dnf. Requires confirmation before execution.
  Examples:
    dbxcli linux package update name=oracle-database-preinstall-19c --target db01

## dbxcli linux security
  Security status checks
  Security status checks: SELinux, firewall rules, and running services over SSH.
  Inherited Flags:
    --format  output format: table, json, yaml
    --target  target name (from ~/.dbx/targets/)

## dbxcli linux security firewall-list
  List firewall rules

## dbxcli linux security selinux-status
  Check SELinux status

## dbxcli linux security service-status
  List running services

## dbxcli linux storage
  Storage and LVM management
  Linux storage and LVM management via pvs/vgs/lvs, lvcreate, df, lsblk over SSH.
  Inherited Flags:
    --format  output format: table, json, yaml
    --target  target name (from ~/.dbx/targets/)

## dbxcli linux storage disk-usage
  Show disk usage

## dbxcli linux storage lv-create
  Create a logical volume (confirm-gated)

## dbxcli linux storage lv-list
  List logical volumes

## dbxcli linux storage pv-list
  List physical volumes

## dbxcli linux storage vg-list
  List volume groups

## dbxcli mcp
  Start MCP server
  Start the dbx MCP (Model Context Protocol) server for AI integration.
  Supports stdio transport (default) and SSE transport for remote connections.
  Flags:
    --port  SSE transport port (0 = stdio) (default: 0)
  Examples:
    dbxcli mcp                  # stdio transport (for Claude Code, IDE extensions)
      dbxcli mcp --port 3001      # SSE transport (for remote MCP clients)

## dbxcli serve
  Start REST API server
  Start the dbx REST API server. Routes are auto-generated from the Cobra command tree.
  The API exposes all CLI operations as HTTP endpoints with JWT authentication.
  Flags:
    --auth-mode  auth mode: jwt, basic, none (default: jwt)
    --port  listen port (default: 8080)
  Examples:
    dbxcli serve
      dbxcli serve --port 9090 --auth-mode basic

## dbxcli target
  Manage system targets
  Manage the target registry — databases, hosts, and services that dbx connects to.
  Targets are stored in ~/.dbx/targets/ as YAML files.
  Inherited Flags:
    --format  output format: table, json, yaml

## dbxcli target add
  Register a new target
  Register a new target (database, host, or service) in the target registry.
  Examples:
    dbxcli target add entity_name=prod-db entity_type=oracle_db host=db01.example.com port=1521 service=ORCL
      dbxcli target add entity_name=web01 entity_type=oracle_host host=web01.example.com

## dbxcli target list
  List registered targets
  List all registered targets with their type, host, and connection status.
  Examples:
    dbxcli target list
      dbxcli target list entity_type=oracle_db

## dbxcli target test
  Test connectivity to a target
  Test network and authentication connectivity to a registered target.
  Examples:
    dbxcli target test entity_name=prod-db
